title: Gate CrossEx: An Exchange-Native Answer to the Institutional Capital Fragmentation Problem
summary: Gate.io's new CrossEx platform lets institutional traders manage funds across exchanges from a single account, reducing inefficiencies. This could make it easier for large traders to move capital quickly and cut costs.
body:
Gate.io has launched CrossEx, a platform designed to help institutional crypto traders manage their funds more efficiently. Currently, large trading desks often struggle with fragmented capital across multiple exchanges, requiring separate accounts and preventing them from netting collateral. This leads to inefficiencies and higher costs.

CrossEx allows traders to consolidate their funds into a single account, enabling them to move capital quickly between exchanges without the need for multiple balance sheets. This could significantly reduce the operational complexity and costs associated with managing funds across different platforms. The platform is particularly useful for active trading desks that need to react quickly to market opportunities.

For everyday crypto users, this development highlights the growing sophistication of institutional trading tools. While retail traders may not directly use CrossEx, the improvements in institutional trading efficiency could lead to better liquidity and more stable markets overall. This could indirectly benefit all traders by reducing volatility and improving market conditions.

Traders and investors should watch for how widely adopted CrossEx becomes and whether other exchanges follow suit with similar solutions. If successful, this could set a new standard for institutional crypto trading, making the market more efficient for everyone. Read more → https://www.theblock.co/post/404882/gate-crossex-an-exchange-native-answer-to-the-institutional-capital-fragmentation-problem?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss
